documents.title: The Two Ordinances — Baptism and Communion
documents.session_topic: Doctrinal exposition of the two church ordinances: Believers Baptism and the Lord's Supper — their biblical basis, mode, recipients, meaning, and local-church practice.

documents.title: Boundaries Talk with the Guys
documents.session_topic: Establishing healthy biblical boundaries for men with the opposite sex — physical, emotional, digital, spiritual, and social safeguards that honor God, protect marriages, and maintain personal purity.

documents.title: Church Governance
documents.session_topic: Biblical pattern of church governance: Lord Jesus as head, elders and deacons as appointed servants, believers as the body; qualifications and responsibility of elders; roles of men and women in the gathering, headship, and head covering.

documents.title: Agreement to Become a Member
documents.session_topic: Outline of the church's formal membership process, distinctives members must affirm, expectations of members, privileges granted, and rationale for instituting formal membership.

documents.title: A Biblical New Testament Perspective on Tithes and Offerings
documents.session_topic: A New Testament-anchored survey of tithes and offerings, arguing that Old Testament tithing does not directly bind the church while the underlying principles of generous, willing giving and supporting ministers and the needy are reaffirmed in the New Testament.
topic_clusters: Biblical Theology (Old Testament / New Testament continuity)

documents.title: On Salvation (Soteriology)
documents.session_topic: Statement of Faith on the doctrine of salvation: a secure gift received by grace through faith and repentance, grounded in Christ's atoning death and resurrection, issuing in adoption and a call to holiness

documents.title: Salvation Words
documents.session_topic: Word study of key salvation-related terms (Grace, Repentance, Faith) with supporting scripture references and discussion points on faith, the heart, and obedience.

documents.title: On Sign Gifts
documents.session_topic: Cessationism vs continuationism regarding the sign gifts of the Spirit (tongues, prophecy, healing, miracles), with arguments for both positions and a personal bridging perspective

documents.title: God (Trinity)
documents.session_topic: Statement of Faith class: doctrine of God as One God eternally existing in three Persons (Trinity), with shared divine attributes and distinct harmonious roles in creation, providence, redemption, and sanctification.

documents.title: Statement of Faith — God the Father
documents.session_topic: Statement of Faith article on God the Father: His identity within the Trinity, eternal nature, role as all-powerful Creator and sustainer, sovereign ordering of all things for His glory, sending of the Son for redemption, and adoption of believers as His children.

documents.title: Statement of Faith — God the Son (Jesus)
documents.session_topic: Statement-of-faith outline on the person and work of Jesus Christ: deity, incarnation, sinlessness, hypostatic union, substitutionary atonement, burial, resurrection, ascension, present priestly intercession, and bodily second coming.

documents.title: SOF Classes - Holy Spirit
documents.session_topic: Statement of Faith class on the Person, nature, attributes, word-pictures, and ministries of the Holy Spirit

documents.title: Statement of Faith — Man (Anthropology)
documents.session_topic: Statement of Faith class on biblical anthropology: God's original design for humanity, the impact of the fall and sin, and the redemptive impact of the cross of Christ.

documents.title: Topics to Discuss in Future (Church Plant Planning Document)
documents.session_topic: Working agenda and decision log for planting a local church: motivations, vision, governance, theological essentials, non-negotiables, finalized positions on contested doctrines (head coverings, sign gifts, eschatology, etc.), and operational to-dos.

documents.title: Vision and Mission of this Fellowship
documents.session_topic: An introductory teaching presenting this fellowship's identity, name etymology, core values, vision, mission, and an invitation to membership and engagement.

top_20_themes.theme: role of women in the church
